stem: In the xy-plane, square JKLM has its diagonals on the x- and y-axes, with vertices at (3, 0), (0, 3), (−3, 0), and (0, −3). What is the area, in square units, of the square?
rationale: Choice A is correct. The diagonals of the square lie along the axes. Each diagonal has length 3 − (−3) = 6 units. For a square with diagonals of length d, the area is d2/2 = 62/2 = 18 square units. Equivalently, the diagonals divide the square into 4 congruent right triangles each with legs of length 3, so the total area is 4 × (1/2)(3)(3) = 18. Choice B is the area of one triangle. Choice C is the perimeter divided by 2. Choice D is the square of the diagonal length.
